diagnoses.name: Myelodysplastic syndrome
diagnoses.evidence_span: significant past medical history including myelodysplastic syndrome

diagnoses.name: Splenic infarction
diagnoses.evidence_span: CT abdomen revealed splenic infarction with associated splenomegaly

diagnoses.name: Acute respiratory failure with hypoxia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Acute respiratory failure with hypoxia secondary to underlying COPD, coalworker's pneumoconiosis, chronic pulmonary edema, and atelectasis

diagnoses.name: Chronic pulmonary edema
diagnoses.evidence_span: CXR demonstrated chronic changes consistent with coalworker's pneumoconiosis with areas of atelectasis bilaterally and evidence of chronic pulmonary edema

diagnoses.name: Acute on chronic diastolic heart failure
diagnoses.evidence_span: Acute on chronic diastolic heart failure exacerbated by hypertensive heart disease

diagnoses.name: Tumor lysis syndrome
diagnoses.evidence_span: Tumor lysis syndrome from myelodysplastic syndrome, managed with aggressive hydration, rasburicase, and allopurinol

diagnoses.name: Acute kidney injury
diagnoses.evidence_span: Acute kidney injury likely multifactorial from sepsis (UTI identified), dehydration, and tumor lysis; creatinine peaked at 2.4

diagnoses.name: Urinary tract infection
diagnoses.evidence_span: Urinary tract infection treated with appropriate antibiotics

diagnoses.name: Neoplasm-related pain
diagnoses.evidence_span: Neoplasm-related pain (acute and chronic) from splenic involvement managed with tramadol

procedures.name: Beam radiation therapy to spleen
procedures.evidence_span: Splenic infarction with massive splenomegaly requiring beam radiation therapy to address pain and size reduction

procedures.name: Drainage of right middle and left lower lung lobes
procedures.evidence_span: managed with drainage of right middle and left lower lung lobes for diagnostic purposes and therapeutic relief

procedures.name: Bone marrow biopsy
procedures.evidence_span: bone marrow biopsy obtained for evaluation

procedures.name: Percutaneous central venous catheter placement
procedures.evidence_span: A percutaneous central venous catheter was placed for future infusion access

medications.name: Tramadol
medications.evidence_span: Tramadol 50 mg PO q6h PRN pain

medications.name: Hydroxyurea
medications.evidence_span: Hydroxyurea 500–1000 mg PO daily (per oncology protocol)

medications.name: Rasburicase
medications.evidence_span: Rasburicase 6 mg IV per protocol for uric acid management

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: Magnesium sulfate 2 gm IV per protocol

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: Potassium chloride 60–80 mEq PO divided dosing

medications.name: Sodium chloride 0.9% flush
medications.evidence_span: Sodium chloride 0.9% flush for central line

medications.name: Glucose gel
medications.evidence_span: Glucose gel 15 g PO PRN hypoglycemia

medications.name: Dextrose 50%
medications.evidence_span: Dextrose 50% IV access PRN severe hypoglycemia

medications.name: Allopurinol
medications.evidence_span: managed with aggressive hydration, rasburicase, and allopurinol

chief_complaint: Progressive dyspnea, abdominal pain, and constitutional symptoms with acute decompensation
